title: Non-text Content
description: All non-text content presented to the user has a text alternative that serves the equivalent purpose.

title: Audio-only and Video-only (Prerecorded)
description: For prerecorded audio-only and video-only media, alternatives are provided.

title: Captions (Prerecorded)
description: Captions are provided for all prerecorded audio content in synchronized media.

title: Audio Description or Media Alternative (Prerecorded)
description: An alternative for time-based media or audio description of the prerecorded video content is provided.

title: Info and Relationships
description: Information, structure, and relationships conveyed through presentation can be programmatically determined.

title: Meaningful Sequence
description: If sequence of content affects meaning, correct reading order can be programmatically determined.

title: Use of Color
description: Color is not the only visual means of conveying information or indicating an action.

title: Contrast (Minimum)
description: Text and images of text have a contrast ratio of at least 4.5:1 (3:1 for large text).

title: Resize Text
description: Text can be resized up to 200% without loss of content or functionality.

title: Contrast (Enhanced)
description: Text and images of text have a contrast ratio of at least 7:1 (4.5:1 for large text).

title: Reflow
description: Content can be presented without loss of information using a width of 320 CSS pixels (no two-dimensional scrolling).

title: Non-text Contrast
description: UI components and graphical objects have a contrast ratio of at least 3:1.

title: Keyboard
description: All functionality is operable through a keyboard interface without requiring specific timings for keystrokes.

title: No Keyboard Trap
description: Focus can be moved away from any component using only the keyboard.

title: Bypass Blocks
description: A mechanism is available to bypass blocks of content that are repeated on multiple pages.

title: Page Titled
description: Web pages have titles that describe topic or purpose.

title: Focus Order
description: Focusable components receive focus in an order that preserves meaning and operability.

title: Link Purpose (In Context)
description: Purpose of each link can be determined from the link text or its programmatic context.

title: Focus Visible
description: Any keyboard operable interface has a mode of operation where the keyboard focus indicator is visible.

title: Language of Page
description: The default human language of each web page can be programmatically determined.

title: On Focus
description: Receiving focus does not initiate a change of context.

title: Error Identification
description: Input errors are identified and described to the user in text.

title: Labels or Instructions
description: Labels or instructions are provided when content requires user input.

title: Parsing
description: Elements have complete start/end tags, are properly nested, and IDs are unique.

title: Name, Role, Value
description: Name, role, and values for UI components can be programmatically determined; states, properties, and values can be set programmatically.
